source.section: AFCS (Automatic Flight Control System) > CPL (Coupled Steering) Mode > CPL WYPT Sub-Mode Example

steps.action: Make sure your stick is centered, no force is applied on it and that you have an appropriate deadzone (see CONTROLS SETUP - WHAT YOU NEED MAPPED section).
Select a desired waypoint using the HSI format.
Activate the WYPT navigation mode by pressing OSB next to WYPT. WYPT is boxed when selected.
If desired, select AUTO to automatically follow the desired waypoint sequence (SEQ1 in our case).
If desired, click and hold (Left/Right mouse button) the CRS (Course Set) knob to set desired course (CSEL) to intercept the waypoint from.
Press the AP button on the UFC to show the autopilot options.
Press the OSB adjacent “CPL” to activate coupled autopilot mode.
A colon will appear (“:CPL”) to indicate that it is active. “CPL WYPT” will appear on both the HSI and HUD.
The autopilot will follow the selected waypoint (or waypoint sequence if AUTO is selected).
You can disengage autopilot by pressing the Paddle Switch on the control stick.
